hero.title: 6 Days — Best of North Vietnam.
hero.titleAccent: Overnight Cruise, Roof of Indochina & Ancient Capital.
hero.intro: The full North Vietnam experience, with no compromise on Halong Bay. An overnight cruise on La Casta — Halong Bay at night, under stars reflected in glassy karst-sheltered water, with a deck of your own and squid fishing under floodlights. Then Sapa: two nights in the highland terrace country with Cat Cat H'Mong Village and Fansipan — Vietnam's highest peak — by cable car. This is the most complete six-day North Vietnam itinerary.
hero.heroImageAlt: Halong Bay overnight cruise La Casta limestone karst Vietnam

overview.heading: 6 Days Best of North Vietnam: Hanoi, Halong Overnight + Sapa
body: This six-day package is the most comprehensive North Vietnam itinerary available, combining an overnight cruise on Halong Bay with two nights in Sapa — covering every essential experience in the north without cutting corners on either destination. It is designed for travellers who want the authentic Halong Bay overnight experience (Tai Chi at sunrise, squid fishing after dinner, kayaking before breakfast) alongside the Sapa highland experience, in a single continuous journey.

The Halong Bay overnight cruise is on the La Casta, Hera, or Amanda cruise vessels — well-maintained 3-star junks with private en-suite cabins, a sea-view restaurant deck, and a kayak fleet. Day two boards at Tuan Chau Harbour, explores the bay through the afternoon (Ba Trai Dao beach, kayaking, Sunset Happy Hour), and anchors for the night in the protected Hoa Cuong Bay area. Day three begins with Tai Chi on deck at 06:15, followed by morning kayaking at Tra Bau lagoon and brunch before disembark.

After returning to Hanoi, the journey continues immediately to Sapa by bus/van the following morning — an early 06:30 pickup for the 5.5-hour drive into the mountains. Two nights in Sapa covers Cat Cat Village on day four and the full Fansipan cable car experience on day five, before the return to Hanoi on day five afternoon.

highlights.heading: Why This 6-Day North Vietnam Overnight Cruise Package Stands Apart

items.title: Halong Bay Overnight Cruise — La Casta/Hera/Amanda
items.body: The overnight experience on Halong Bay is fundamentally different from a day cruise. After the other boats have departed in the evening, the bay settles into a quiet that is impossible to experience from shore. The cruise vessel anchors in a sheltered cove surrounded by karst formations. Evening activities: cooking demonstration of Vietnamese spring rolls, a seafood dinner by the water, and squid fishing by floodlight (squid come to the surface attracted by light, and the technique requires no fishing experience). The morning begins with Tai Chi on the sun deck at 06:15, followed by kayaking at Tra Bau lagoon before brunch and disembark.
items.imageAlt: Halong Bay overnight cruise cabin deck sunset karst Vietnam

items.title: Ba Trai Dao Beach Kayaking
items.body: Ba Trai Dao (Three Peach Island) is a small beach within the bay's inner waters, accessible only by the cruise's kayaks. The shallow turquoise water is calm, the surrounding karst formations create a sheltered cove, and the beach itself is a natural arc of fine white sand with no facilities — just the karst, the water, and your kayak. The kayaking here in the afternoon light is one of the best single experiences in the Halong Bay circuit.
items.imageAlt: Ba Trai Dao beach kayaking Halong Bay limestone karst Vietnam

items.title: Fansipan Summit + Buddhist Spiritual Sites
items.body: Fansipan at 3,147 metres is the Roof of Indochina — the highest peak in Vietnam, Laos, and Cambodia combined. The Sun World complex makes it accessible via the Muong Hoa Hiking Train and the world's longest three-rope gondola cable car. At the summit, 11 Buddhist sites include the 21.5-metre Great Buddha (cast from 50 tons of copper), the 12-metre Guan Yin statue (18 tons), and the prayer halls and shrines built into the mountain's upper slopes. Fansipan is as much a spiritual site as a summit view.
items.imageAlt: Fansipan Great Buddha cable car summit Sapa North Vietnam

items.title: Cat Cat Village — Black H'Mong Community
items.body: Cat Cat Village is the most accessible H'Mong cultural destination in Sapa — a 30-minute walking trail from the town centre down through terraced rice paddies to a Black H'Mong settlement. Women at the entrance sell hand-dyed indigo fabrics and silver jewellery made to traditional designs. The village waterfall feeds the terraced paddies below. On clear afternoons, the view from the terrace above Cat Cat takes in the Muong Hoa Valley's full extent — the same valley through which the Fansipan cable car ascends.
items.imageAlt: Cat Cat Village Black H'Mong rice terraces Sapa North Vietnam

itinerary.heading: Your 6-Day Best of North Vietnam Itinerary

days.title: Arrive Hanoi — City Tour
days.description: Arrive at Noi Bai International Airport. Hotel check-in and full Hanoi city tour: Tran Quoc Pagoda, Ba Dinh Square, Ho Chi Minh Mausoleum, Hoan Kiem Lake, Hanoi Opera House, Old Quarter (36 Ancient Streets). Pho and egg coffee tasting en route. Evening dinner at a Cha Ca grilled fish restaurant — turmeric-marinated fish cooked at the table with dill and spring onions.
suggestedActivities: Noi Bai Airport arrival and hotel check-in
Ho Chi Minh Mausoleum and Ba Dinh Square
Hoan Kiem Lake and Ngoc Son Temple

days.title: Halong Bay Overnight Cruise — Board at Tuan Chau
days.description: 08:30: Transfer to Tuan Chau Harbour (150 km, 2.5 hours). Board La Casta, Hera, or Amanda Cruise. Cruise through Hoa Cuong Bay past the karst formations (Dog Rock, Duck Rock, Thumb Rock). Buffet lunch on board. Ba Trai Dao Beach: kayaking and swimming in shallow karst-sheltered cove. Sunset Party Happy Hour (buy 2 get 1 on drinks). Vietnamese cooking demonstration — spring roll preparation. Seafood dinner on the restaurant deck. Squid fishing by floodlight. Karaoke on deck. Overnight at anchor in Hoa Cuong Bay.
suggestedActivities: Transfer to Tuan Chau Harbour (2.5 hrs)

days.title: Halong Bay Morning — Return Hanoi
days.description: 06:15: Tai Chi practice on the sun deck at sunrise, with karst formations surrounding the anchored vessel. Morning kayaking at Tra Bau Lagoon — a narrow waterway through the karst limestone for a 30-minute paddle. Brunch on board. 11:30: Disembark at Tuan Chau Harbour. Return journey to Hanoi (150 km, 2.5 hours). Arrive Hanoi afternoon. Dinner at own arrangement.
suggestedActivities: Morning kayaking at Tra Bau Lagoon

days.title: Drive to Sapa — Cat Cat Village
days.description: 06:30: Early pickup for Sapa drive (320 km, 5.5 hours via Lao Cai expressway). Arrive Sapa midday. Hotel check-in and lunch. Afternoon: guided walk to Cat Cat Village — 30 minutes downhill through terraced paddy fields to the Black H'Mong settlement. Village waterfall, traditional weaving, optional Rainbow Slide (approximately RM10 own expense). Return to hotel for dinner.
suggestedActivities: Drive to Sapa (5.5 hrs with breaks)

days.title: Fansipan Cable Car — Return Hanoi
days.description: 08:00: Meet guide at Sun Plaza Station. Muong Hoa Hiking Train to Sun World Fansipan Legend. Full Fansipan combo included: cable car to 3,143m, funicular to peak, all entrance fees, buffet lunch. At the summit: Great Buddha (21.5m), Guan Yin statue, 11 Buddhist sites. Panoramic views across Hoang Lien Son National Park. Allow 4+ hours at the complex. Afternoon: return to Sapa and transfer to Hanoi (5 hours). Arrive Hanoi late evening. Dinner at own arrangement.
suggestedActivities: Muong Hoa Train from Sun Plaza
Great Buddha and Guan Yin statues
Buffet lunch at Van Sam Restaurant

days.title: Hanoi Free — Depart
days.description: Free morning in Hanoi. Optional: Temple of Literature (Hanoi's first university, 1070), Hoa Lo Prison Museum, or a final Old Quarter walk. Transfer to Noi Bai International Airport per your agreed schedule.

whyBook.heading: North Vietnam's Most Complete Experience — By Malaysia's Trusted Agency Since 1988
whyBook.body: This is the package we recommend to Malaysian travellers who are visiting Vietnam for the first time and want to see all of it. The overnight cruise provides an Halong Bay experience that day trips simply cannot match — the quality of light on the karst at sunrise, the silence of the bay at night, the squid fishing. Dhesu has been arranging this circuit since 1988. We select cruise vessels we have personally inspected and have been using for years.

points.title: MATTA & IATA Registered
points.body: Fully licensed Malaysian travel agency. Our Halong Bay overnight cruise selection is based on years of firsthand inspection and consistent feedback from Malaysian travellers.

points.title: 35+ Years of Vietnam Experience
points.body: Since 1988, we have been running North Vietnam circuits for Malaysian travellers. Our Halong cruise partners, Hanoi guides, and Sapa contacts are trusted long-term relationships.

points.title: Overnight Cruise Fully Included
points.body: All meals on the cruise (lunch + seafood dinner + brunch), all onboard activities (cooking demo, squid fishing, Tai Chi, kayaking), and all entrance fees are included. No hidden costs on the water.

points.title: Buy 3 Free 1 Promotion
points.body: Groups of 4 travelling together pay for 3 — the 4th person travels free. Confirm this offer at the time of booking.

sections.heading: 6 Day Best of North Vietnam Tour from Malaysia with Overnight Halong Cruise
sections.body:
The 6-day Best of North Vietnam tour with overnight Halong Bay cruise is the premium North Vietnam itinerary for Malaysian travellers. An overnight cruise delivers the Halong Bay experience that no day trip can replicate — the evening atmosphere, the sunrise light on the karst, the onboard activities, and the genuine sense of being on the water in one of Asia's most extraordinary landscapes.

Combined with two nights in Sapa (Cat Cat Village and the full Fansipan Sun World cable car experience), and the Hanoi city tour on day one, this six-day package covers every essential sight in North Vietnam without compromise. ETIQA travel insurance for 6–10 day trips is available from RM60 per person.

faq.heading: Questions About the 6-Day Best of North Vietnam Package

items.question: What cruise vessel is used for the Halong Bay overnight?
items.answer: The 3-star tier uses the Hera or Amanda cruise vessels — traditional junk-style boats with private en-suite cabins, a covered restaurant deck, and a kayak fleet. The 4-star deluxe and 5-star tiers use La Casta Cruise, which has larger cabins and an upgraded restaurant. All vessels are well-maintained and regularly inspected. The cruise departs from Tuan Chau Harbour.

items.question: What happens on the Halong Bay cruise?
items.answer: Day 2 (boarding): Buffet lunch, Ba Trai Dao Beach kayaking, Sunset Happy Hour, cooking demonstration (Vietnamese spring rolls), seafood dinner, squid fishing, karaoke. Day 3 (morning): Tai Chi at sunrise, kayaking at Tra Bau Lagoon, brunch, disembark at 11:30. All onboard activities are included in the package.

items.question: Do Malaysians need a visa for Vietnam?
items.answer: No. Malaysian passport holders enter Vietnam visa-free for up to 30 days. No advance application required.

items.question: How does this package differ from the 6-day Hanoi Halong Sapa package?
items.answer: The main difference is the Halong Bay format: this package includes an overnight cruise on Halong Bay (staying on the cruise vessel overnight), while the 6-day Hanoi Halong Sapa package (pkgid 2165) uses a day cruise. The overnight experience adds the squid fishing, Tai Chi at sunrise, and the evening atmosphere on the bay. This package is approximately RM300 more per person than the day-cruise version.
